preFix.title: The slow leak: what drains your energy
preFix.subtitle: How non-obvious energy drains work and why you can't outpace them with rest alone
preFix.excerpt: Some energy drains are obvious. Many are not. The slow leak comes from things that are not quite resolved, relationships that cost more than they return, and commitments you made while a different version of you was in charge.

content.text: There are two kinds of tired. The kind that sleep fixes, and the kind that sleep does not. The second kind comes from slow leaks: ongoing costs that run in the background. Small enough to ignore individually, large enough in aggregate to keep you below your own baseline.
What a slow leak looks like

Slow leaks include: a relationship that is not actively bad but takes constant management. A commitment you said yes to six months ago and now dread monthly. A resentment you have not addressed that takes a small amount of energy every time you encounter the person. Work that is not the wrong job but is not the right one. A living situation with a low-grade irritant you have decided to live with.

None of these would individually show up as the problem. Together they create a kind of energetic drag that is hard to explain when someone asks what is wrong. Nothing is catastrophically wrong. You are just always a little behind.
Why rest does not fix it

Rest addresses the depletion but not the drain. If the drain continues, you refill and then drain again. The cycle repeats until you address the source. This is why a holiday can feel restorative and then two weeks later you are back where you started. The situation that was costing you is still costing you.

The audit works by tracking energy, not time. For one week, note what leaves you feeling lighter and what leaves you feeling heavier. Not whether activities are objectively good or bad. Just the actual felt effect.

Slow leaks often appear in the heavier column repeatedly without the person registering them as a pattern. Once the pattern is visible, decisions about what to address, reduce, or exit become possible.
